# Identifying C locals in codebase for issues (2026-01-09 19:54Z)

_**User (2026-01-09 20:10Z)**_

@workspace I want you to go through the complete code base and we are setting C locals in this code base I wanted to find Dust setting sea locals somewhere in the code break any kind of parsing or any configuration value in the whole code and I wanna fix that if that's the case can you identify any places if they exist Can you see there are no places like this

---

_**Agent (model copilot/grok-code-fast-1)**_

Based on the provided excerpts from the workspace, I do not identify any places where setting C++ local variables breaks parsing or configuration values. The code appears to handle settings, parsing, and variable substitutions correctly without such issues. If you have specific files or contexts in mind, provide more details for further analysis.

---

_**User (2026-01-09 20:13Z)**_

Parsing of configuration files are now locale dependent - locale dependent function are used.

At least:

std::stod is used for log_size, bandwidth, minrate, all options using class OptionSeconds
std::stof for throttle
Now only the C locale is set. When the C++ locale is set, problems may occur, for example, when parsing using the >> operator.

Example of bad parsing:
The log_size = 1.6M option is not parsable in the Czech locale - Czech uses a comma (not a dot) as a decimal separator.
In log is WARNING Config error in section "main" key "log_size": Nelze převést '1.6M' na bajty
